question: The quoted price for a certain part is 35 euros per unit for a batch of 10 units. The part can be produced in the company's own plant with the following costs: 14 euros the unit raw material, 12 euros the direct labor, labor overhead at 150%, 6 euros for equipment fixed cost. Should the part be bought or made in-house? Calculations:

options.A: to produce because the production costs would be higher than those involved in the purchase
options.B: to produce because the production costs would be lower than those involved in the purchase
options.C: to buy because the production costs would be lower than those involved in the purchase
options.D: to buy because the production costs would be higher than those involved in the purchase

question: Which of the following statements is/are true?

options.A: Through their activity the factories have no impact on the environment.
options.B: Through their activity not all the factories have an impact on the environment.
options.C: Through their activity the factories have an impact on the environment

question: Which of the following activities in a modern manufacturing system rely on the use of computers?
Which of the following are typical for Industry 4.0?
Which of the following are characteristic of modern manufacturing systems

options.A: production is efficient only if it is done in small batches
options.B: production is also efficient if done in small batches
options.D: the control system is generally hierarchical

question: Production in large batches is mainly used in the case of a modern manufacturing system:
What are the consequences of manufacturing resource planning (MRP II)?

options.A: more money spent on investments for material
options.B: reductions in the amount of inventory declared obsolete

question: Which of the following are related to design for life cycle?

options.A: the more homogeneous the groups along the main diagonal are (i.e., the more '1's it contains), the more efficiently the machines are allocated.
options.B: the more homogeneous the groups on the anti-diagonal are (i.e., the more '1's they contain), the more efficiently the machines can be allocated.
options.C: the more heterogeneous the groups along the main diagonal are, the more efficiently the machines are allocated.
options.D: the more heterogeneous the groups on the anti-diagonal are, the more efficiently the machines can be allocated.

question: What is the most modern vehicle guidance technology used in a factory?

options.A: is used to convert the aggregate production plan into a master production schedule.
options.B: involves planning the production output levels for major production lines.
options.C: translate the MPS of end products into a detailed schedule for the raw materials and parts used in those end products.
options.D: determines the labor and equipment resources needed to achieve the master schedule.

question: Which of the following methods are not specific to green production?

options.C: increase the number of materials required for a product
options.D: extend the life cycle of a product

options.A: involves a large amount of data collected from sensors
options.B: aims to ensure that production is not affected by equipment failure
options.C: requires less data acquired from the production system
